representative_artifacts.observation: Inspected synchronized frames near 0.5 s, 3.0 s, and 5.9 s. The segmented disc and coral registration wedge visibly rotate in every cell; the three ejection cases leave the support, while mu=0.5, omega=2 remains supported through 6 s. Labels are legible, full disc margins remain visible, and no contact-force arrows appear.

The four author configurations appear in source order with explicit parameter and selected-time labels. Each selected still keeps the cube visible near the support rim and the coral wedge provides an unambiguous angular registration mark.
At 2.27 s the low-friction, low-speed cube is still visible at the far rim immediately before the later clip frames show it off the support; the disc is fully framed and no force arrows are present.
At 2.00 s the low-friction, high-speed cube is visibly at the far rim immediately before its subsequent departure; the segmented support and registration wedge remain clear.

At the 6.00 s horizon the mu=0.5, omega=2 cube remains visibly supported. This observation establishes retention through the horizon only and does not assert zero radial slip, perfect sticking, or co-rotation.
At 1.50 s the high-friction, high-speed cube is still visible at the far rim; later synchronized clip frames show it off the support, making the ejection outcome directly observable.
